+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Lilith Zero — Azure App Service deploy script
+# Run from repo root: bash scripts/azure-deploy.sh
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# ── Config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+RESOURCE_GROUP="lilith-zero-rg"
+LOCATION="westeurope"
+APP_SERVICE_PLAN="lilith-zero-plan"
+APP_NAME="lilith-zero-webhook"           # → lilith-zero-webhook.azurewebsites.net
+REGISTRY_NAME="lilithzerocr"             # Azure Container Registry (must be globally unique)
+IMAGE_NAME="lilith-zero"
+IMAGE_TAG="latest"
+SUBSCRIPTION="278ef486-791b-47a4-b61b-decefe29f308"
+TENANT_ID="98e2f7d2-c1d3-4410-b87f-2396f157975f"
+APP_ID="b74dfc6e-544a-4ae1-89f7-6597ebf79edc"
+
+echo "=== Lilith Zero Azure Deploy ==="
+echo ""
+
+# ── 0. Set subscription ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+az account set --subscription "$SUBSCRIPTION"
+echo "✓ Subscription: $SUBSCRIPTION"
+
+# ── 1. Resource Group ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Creating resource group $RESOURCE_GROUP in $LOCATION..."
+az group create --name "$RESOURCE_GROUP" --location "$LOCATION" --output none
+echo "✓ Resource group ready"
+
+# ── 2. Container Registry ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Creating Azure Container Registry $REGISTRY_NAME..."
+az acr create \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --name "$REGISTRY_NAME" \
+    --sku Basic \
+    --admin-enabled true \
+    --output none
+echo "✓ Container registry ready"
+
+# ── 3. Build & push Docker image ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Building and pushing Docker image..."
+az acr build \
+    --registry "$REGISTRY_NAME" \
+    --image "${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" \
+    --file Dockerfile \
+    . 
+echo "✓ Image pushed: ${REGISTRY_NAME}.azurecr.io/${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
+
+# ── 4. App Service Plan (B1 = cheapest with custom domain + SSL) ─────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Creating App Service Plan (B1)..."
+az appservice plan create \
+    --name "$APP_SERVICE_PLAN" \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --is-linux \
+    --sku B1 \
+    --output none
+echo "✓ App Service Plan ready (B1 Linux)"
+
+# ── 5. Web App ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Creating Web App $APP_NAME..."
+ACR_LOGIN_SERVER="${REGISTRY_NAME}.azurecr.io"
+ACR_PASSWORD=$(az acr credential show --name "$REGISTRY_NAME" --query "passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
+
+az webapp create \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --plan "$APP_SERVICE_PLAN" \
+    --name "$APP_NAME" \
+    --deployment-container-image-name "${ACR_LOGIN_SERVER}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" \
+    --docker-registry-server-url "https://${ACR_LOGIN_SERVER}" \
+    --docker-registry-server-user "$REGISTRY_NAME" \
+    --docker-registry-server-password "$ACR_PASSWORD" \
+    --output none
+echo "✓ Web app created: https://${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
+
+# ── 6. App Settings (env vars for the container) ─────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Configuring app settings..."
+az webapp config appsettings set \
+    --name "$APP_NAME" \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --settings \
+        RUST_LOG=info \
+        WEBSITES_ENABLE_APP_SERVICE_STORAGE=true \
+        WEBSITES_PORT=8080 \
+    --output none
+echo "✓ App settings configured"
+
+# ── 7. Persistent storage for session taint files ────────────────────────────
+# Azure App Service /home is already persistent across restarts for Linux containers.
+# The binary writes to /home/.lilith/sessions/ which maps to persistent storage.
+echo "✓ Session persistence: /home/.lilith/sessions (Azure persistent /home)"
+
+# ── 8. Custom domain: lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz ─────────────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Adding custom domain lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz..."
+echo ""
+echo "  ⚠  ACTION REQUIRED IN NAMECHEAP:"
+echo "  Change the CNAME for 'lilith-zero' from:"
+echo "    lingo-popsicle-bulgur.ngrok-free.dev"
+echo "  To:"
+echo "    ${APP_NAME}.azurewebsites.net"
+echo ""
+echo "  After you update it, press ENTER to continue..."
+read -r
+
+az webapp config hostname add \
+    --webapp-name "$APP_NAME" \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --hostname "lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz" \
+    --output none
+echo "✓ Custom hostname added"
+
+# ── 9. Managed certificate (free SSL for custom domain) ──────────────────────
+echo ""
+echo "Creating free managed SSL certificate for lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz..."
+az webapp config ssl create \
+    --resource-group "$RESOURCE_GROUP" \
+    --name "$APP_NAME" \
+    --hostname "lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz" \
+    --output none 2>/dev/null || echo "(cert may take a few minutes to provision — DNS propagation)"
body: This only requests the managed certificate. Azure App Service still needs an explicit SSL binding step for the custom hostname, otherwise `https://lilith-zero.badcompany.xyz` will continue serving without the managed cert attached and the Power Platform HTTPS validation will fail.

If `read_dir` fails here (for example because the directory exists but App Service permissions or mount state are wrong), the server silently continues with zero loaded policies and only logs `Loaded 0...`. That turns a startup misconfiguration into runtime deny-all behavior instead of surfacing the deployment failure immediately.

The default info log now includes the full tool arguments for every webhook request. Those arguments can contain sensitive business data or secrets from `inputValues`, so this change starts leaking request contents into normal production logs even when `LILITH_ZERO_WEBHOOK_DEBUG` is false.

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**`policies/*` only covers one directory level — nested policy subdirectories will be silently excluded.**

`!examples/copilot_studio/policies/*` matches only files directly inside `policies/`. If Cedar policy files are organized into subdirectories (e.g., `policies/agents/foo.cedar`), those files are excluded from the Docker build context without any build error — the container simply starts with fewer policies.

<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 !examples/copilot_studio/policies
-!examples/copilot_studio/policies/*
+!examples/copilot_studio/policies/**
```
</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`For the Excel Online (Business) connector in Copilot Studio/Power Automate, what inputs are required for the `CreateTable` operation, and are literal strings like `locationG`, `doc_libG`, `fileG`, and `table_rangeG` valid workbook identifiers or just placeholders?`

💡 Result:

The required inputs for the CreateTable operation in the Excel Online (Business) connector are: source (string, required), drive (string, required), file (string, required), Range (string, required). Optional inputs are TableName (string) and ColumnsNames (string). In the UI, these map to Location (or source/drive), Document Library (doc_lib), File (file), Table Range (table_range), Table Name, and Column Names. Literal strings like locationG, doc_libG, fileG, and table_rangeG are placeholders (likely example or generated variable names with a 'G' suffix, common in some UIs for generative inputs), not valid workbook identifiers. Valid values are actual locations like "me" or SharePoint URLs for source/drive, actual library names for doc_lib, file paths/IDs for file, and A1 notation ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1'!A1:B10) for table_range.

Citations:

- 1: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/connectors/excelonlinebusiness/
- 2: https://grokipedia.com/page/Create_table_Power_Automate
- 3: https://www.powertechtips.com/create-excel-table-in-worksheet-power-automate/

---



</details>

**Replace the literal Excel placeholders with real bindings.**

The inputs `source`, `drive`, `file`, and `Range` are set to placeholder strings (`locationG`, `doc_libG`, `fileG`, `table_rangeG`) that are not valid workbook identifiers. The `CreateTable` operation requires actual values: location/SharePoint URLs for `source`/`drive`, actual library names for `doc_lib`, file paths or IDs for `file`, and A1 notation ranges (e.g., `'Sheet1'!A1:B10`) for `Range`. With placeholder values, this action cannot execute against a real workbook.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@debug_bot.yaml` around lines 413 - 428, The CreateTable action's
ManualTaskInput entries use placeholder strings (propertyName: source, drive,
file, Range) — replace these with real workbook bindings: set source to the
actual location/SharePoint URL for the workbook, drive to the correct document
library name or drive id, file to the file path or file id of the workbook, and
Range to a valid A1 notation range (e.g., 'Sheet1'!A1:B10); update the
ManualTaskInput values for source, drive, file, and Range in the inputs block so
the CreateTable operation can target a real workbook.
```

</details>

`For the Excel Online (Business) connector in Copilot Studio/Power Automate, what inputs are required for the `GetTables` operation?`

💡 Result:

The required inputs for the GetTables operation in the Excel Online (Business) connector are: - source (string, required): Specifies the drive or location, such as "me", "SharePoint Site URL", "users/someone's UPN", "groups/group Id", or "sites/SharePoint Site URL:/teams/team name:". - drive (string, required): The ID of the drive. - file (string, required): The ID or path of the Excel file. Optional inputs include: - extractSensitivityLabel (boolean) - fetchSensitivityLabelMetadata (boolean)
